scene_table: Vitality and drive for the work
m1_quick: Citrine, pyrite, jade, aventurine, tiger eye, bloodstone, and ruby are among the most commonly carried crystals for money. Many people keep them where they work or handle finances as symbolic cues — reminders to notice opportunity, act, and put in the work. Crystals are symbolic objects for intention-setting, not promises of wealth; real money comes from effort, skills, and sometimes professional advice.
m2_understanding: Money is a touchy subject — most people carry some worry about it, and the worry rarely helps. What does help is a clearer stance: noticing opportunity, acting on it, putting in steady effort, getting advice when needed. A small, tangible cue can mark that stance — a stone kept where you plan or work, a reminder that money is something you build, not something that simply arrives.
m25_chose: We chose these seven because they speak to the most common money-related intentions in crystal tradition: a wealth mindset, good fortune, opportunity, willpower, and the drive income work asks for. Their meanings come from tradition and personal practice, not any promise of financial return.
m5_use: Keep it where you handle money. On a desk or by a budget, as a wealth-mindset cue.
Carry it into work. A pocket stone as a reminder to put in the effort.
Hold it while setting a money goal. A few breaths to sharpen one intention.
Pair it with action. The stone marks the mindset; the work has to follow.
m6_combos: Pairing is about layering intentions. A popular combination is Citrine + Pyrite — a wealth mindset paired with the drive to act. Aventurine + Tiger Eye is another, chosen for spotting opportunity and the nerve to pursue it.
m8_intro: Explore genuine natural crystal pieces carried for the work of money — stones, bracelets, and forms you can keep where you work or plan, spanning the seven crystals above.
closing: Money is built, not wished into being — and a real stone kept where you work can be a steady cue to do the building. Start with one and let effort add up. For more, explore Abundance & Success.

faq.q: Can crystals bring me money or make me rich?
faq.a: No. Crystals are symbolic objects for intention-setting; they cannot bring money or wealth. Real income comes from effort, skills, planning, and sometimes professional advice. The stone is a cue, not a cause.

faq.q: Are crystals scientifically proven to attract money?
faq.a: No. There is no evidence that crystals attract money or wealth. Many people carry them as mindset cues; any benefit is in the attention and action they prompt, not a proven effect of the stone.

faq.q: Which crystal is best for money and a wealth mindset?
faq.a: Citrine is the most commonly chosen — long tied to abundance and a warm, opportunity-spotting outlook.

faq.q: What is the best crystal for good fortune with money?
faq.a: Jade and aventurine — jade as a steady good-fortune stone, aventurine for spotting new opportunities.

faq.q: Which crystal helps me stay driven while building income?
faq.a: Pyrite and tiger eye — pyrite for the willpower to keep going, tiger eye for steady push toward a goal.

faq.q: How should I cleanse money crystals?
faq.a: Smoke (sage, palo santo), sound (singing bowl), or moonlight work for most stones. Pyrite is metallic and can tarnish if damp — wipe it dry; keep jade and aventurine out of long soaks.

faq.q: What is the best crystal bracelet for money?
faq.a: A citrine bracelet is the most common first choice — warm, widely available, and easy to wear daily as a reminder to notice opportunity and act.
